rationale: The law mandates bias audits for automated employment tools. This control explicitly requires the establishment of an Ethics Committee to review projects and the execution of pre-deployment bias testing using demographic metrics to detect discrimination.

NYC Local Law 144 requires an independent bias audit. This control operationalizes the engagement of external auditors to obtain independent assurance over AI governance and fairness controls ensuring the audit is distinct from internal operations.

To pass a bias audit organizations must demonstrate active mitigation. This control mandates the definition of fairness metrics (e.g. demographic parity) and the documentation of data interventions and constraints used to reduce unjustified bias across the model lifecycle.

Results of bias audits must be published publicly. This control governs the management of public communications and specifically requires that AI-related transparency disclosures meet jurisdictional expectations such as the public summary required by NYC.

Candidates must be notified of AI use. This control requires the publication of explainability statements and disclosures where AI is used supporting the notice requirements inherent in the regulation.
